---
title: ""
toc: false
image: assets/og-image.png
open-graph:
  title: "ICEMAN"
  description: "A structured instrument for assessing the credibility of subgroup effects in randomized trials and meta-analyses."
  type: website
twitter-card:
  title: "ICEMAN"
  description: "A structured instrument for assessing the credibility of subgroup effects in randomized trials and meta-analyses."
  card-style: summary
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-intro">
  <h1>ICEMAN</h1>
  <p class="hero-subtitle">Instrument to assess the Credibility of Effect Modification ANalyses</p>
</div>
<div class="hero">
  <p class="lead">Quick links:</p>
  <div class="hero-cards-row hero-cards-top">
    <a href="tool.qmd" class="hero-card">
      <div class="hero-card-icon">📋</div>
      <div class="hero-card-title">ICEMAN online</div>
      <div class="hero-card-sub">Interactive assessment forms</div>
    </a>
    <a href="manual/index.qmd" class="hero-card">
      <div class="hero-card-icon">📘</div>
      <div class="hero-card-title">User manual</div>
      <div class="hero-card-sub">Guidance with examples</div>
    </a>
  </div>
  <div class="hero-cards-row hero-cards-bottom">
    <a href="files/ICEMAN RCT 1.1 (6).docx" class="hero-card">
      <div class="hero-card-icon">📄</div>
      <div class="hero-card-title">Download ICEMAN for RCTs</div>
      <div class="hero-card-sub">Version 1.1 .docx-file</div>
    </a>
    <a href="files/ICEMAN MA 1.1 (8).docx" class="hero-card">
      <div class="hero-card-icon">📄</div>
      <div class="hero-card-title">Download ICEMAN for Meta-analyses</div>
      <div class="hero-card-sub">Version 1.1 .docx-file</div>
    </a>
    <a href="files/ICEMAN table templates.docx" class="hero-card">
      <div class="hero-card-icon">📄</div>
      <div class="hero-card-title">Reporting templates</div>
      <div class="hero-card-sub">Different summary tables, .docx-file</div>
    </a>
  </div>
</div>
```

## What is ICEMAN?

ICEMAN is a formal instrument for assessing the credibility of possible effect modification, also called a subgroup effect. It helps users judge whether an apparent subgroup effect is likely to be real, or a product of chance or bias. ICEMAN uses structured questions and response options and produces an overall credibility rating from **very low** to **high**.

ICEMAN supports several users and use cases. Trial investigators and systematic reviewers can use it to interpret and report a subgroup effect they found in their study or review. Systematic reviewers can use it to assess subgroup effects reported in included studies. Clinicians, patients, peer reviewers, editors, and others can use it when critically reading a study report that claims a subgroup effect.

We developed ICEMAN for randomized controlled trials and meta-analyses of randomized controlled trials, but users have also found the instrument helpful for other study types, such as cohort studies or meta-analyses of observational studies. For these applications, users should reflect the additional risk of bias in the overall credibility rating.

When reporting an ICEMAN assessment in a paper, users can download the fillable form for [RCTs](files/ICEMAN RCT 1.1 (6).docx) or [meta-analyses](files/ICEMAN MA 1.1 (8).docx) and include it in the appendix of their publication. Alternatively, they can complete the [tool online](tool.qmd) and download a reporting table. We also provide [templates](files/ICEMAN table templates.docx) for summarizing several assessments in a single table. A detailed manual is available as [HTML](manual/index.qmd) and [PDF](files/ICEMAN%20manual%20.pdf).


## Which version should I use?

- Use **ICEMAN for RCTs** to assess an apparent subgroup effect in a single randomized controlled trial.
- Use **ICEMAN for meta-analyses** to assess an apparent subgroup effect in a systematic review or meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.


## How to cite

```{=html}
<div class="cite-box">
  <strong>Cite ICEMAN as:</strong><br><br>
  Schandelmaier S, Briel M, Varadhan R, Schmid CH, Devasenapathy N, Hayward RA,
  Gagnier JJ, Borenstein M, VanDerHeijden GJMG, Dahabreh IJ, Sun X, Sauerbrei W,
  Walsh M, Ioannidis JPA, Thabane L, Guyatt GH.
  Development of a tool to assess the credibility of subgroup analyses (ICEMAN)
  in randomized trials and systematic reviews.
  <em>CMAJ</em> 2020;192(32):E901–E906.
  <a href="https://doi.org/10.1503/cmaj.200077" target="_blank">https://doi.org/10.1503/cmaj.200077</a>
  <br><br>
  <button class="btn btn-sm btn-outline-secondary copy-btn"
    onclick="navigator.clipboard.writeText('Schandelmaier S, et al. Development of a tool to assess the credibility of subgroup analyses (ICEMAN) in randomized trials and systematic reviews. CMAJ 2020;192(32):E901–E906. https://doi.org/10.1503/cmaj.200077'); this.textContent='Copied!'; setTimeout(()=>this.textContent='Copy',2000)">
    Copy citation
  </button>
</div>
```

See [about page](about.qmd#ongoing-projects) for other publications, endorsements, and related ongoing projects.

---

```{=html}
<footer class="site-footer">
  <span>Last updated: May 2026</span>
  <span class="footer-sep">·</span>
  <a href="mailto:s.schandelmaier@gmail.com">Contact</a>
</footer>
```
